revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Shinto shrine]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24744680692|commit triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Hioki-jinja(Tachibana 1-chōme, Nagoya)]][[ja:日置神社 (名古屋市)]] {{Expand Japanese|日置神社 (名古屋市)}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Hioki Shrine (Nagoya) | native_name = 日置神社 (名古屋市) | image = Hioki-jinja Tachibana ac (4).jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Hioki Shrine (Nagoya) | coordinates = {{coord|35.15518|136.899539}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | deity = {{ill|Futodama|lt=Futodama|en|Futodama|it|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|ru|Футодама|simple|Futodama|zh|布刀玉命|qid=Q11059539|1=Futodama}} }} {{nihongo|'''Hioki Shrine (Nagoya)'''|日置神社 (名古屋市)}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Owari Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Q134917287|ltq=Q134917287|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q11390939|ltq=Q11390939|qid=Q11390939|1=Hachiman shrine}} * {{ill|Kokuhei-sha|lt=Kokuhei-sha|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Hiteisha|lt=Shikinai Hiteisha|ja|比定社|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11465292|ltq=Q11465292|qid=Q11465292|1=List of Shikinaisha in Owari Province}} ** Series ordinal: 102 ** P155: {{ill|Q135039545|ltq=Q135039545|qid=Q135039545|1=Ishitsukurino Shrine (Q135039545)}} ** P156: {{ill|Kamutsuchikamano Shrine|lt=Kamutsuchikamano Shrine|qid=Q135039546|1=Kamutsuchikamano Shrine}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q66104246|ltq=Q66104246|qid=Q66104246|1=Tachibana 1-chōme}} * {{ill|Q514506|ltq=Q514506|qid=Q514506|1=Owari Province}} * {{ill|Aichi District|lt=Aichi district|ceb|Aichi-gun|de|Aichi-gun|en|Aichi District|es|Distrito de Aichi (Aichi)|fa|شهرستان آیچی، آیچی|fr|District d'Aichi|hu|Aicsi körzet|id|Distrik Aichi, Aichi|it|Distretto di Aichi|ja|愛知郡 (愛知県)|ko|아이치군|pl|Powiat Aichi|ru|Айти (уезд)|tl|Distrito ng Aichi, Aichi|uk|Повіт Айті|vi|Aichi (huyện)|zh|愛知郡 (愛知縣)|qid=Q552687|1=Aichi District}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.15518|136.899539}} == image (P18) == * Hioki-jinja Tachibana ac (4).jpg == dedicated to (P825) == * {{ill|Futodama|lt=Futodama|en|Futodama|it|Futodama|ja|フトダマ|ru|Футодама|simple|Futodama|zh|布刀玉命|qid=Q11059539|1=Futodama}} * {{ill|Q261637|ltq=Q261637|qid=Q261637|1=Hachiman}} == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181442 ** P3831: {{ill|Q135159299|ltq=Q135159299|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == modern shrine ranking (P13723) == * {{ill|Gō-sha|lt=Gō-sha|ja|郷社|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} ** End time: {'time': '+1946-02-02T00:00:00Z', 'timezone': 0, 'before': 0, 'after': 0, 'precision': 11, 'calendarmodel': 'http://www.wikidata.org/entity/Q1985727'} ** P1534: {{ill|Q3029647|ltq=Q3029647|qid=Q3029647|1=Shinto Directive}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 5180005000320 == Commons category (P373) == * Hioki-jinja(Tachibana 1-chōme, Nagoya) == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '愛知県名古屋市中区橘一丁目3番21号', 'language': 'ja'} {{commons category|Category:Hioki-jinja(Tachibana 1-chōme, Nagoya)}} [[JA:日置神社 (名古屋市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11510248}} == Japanese content == {{出典の明記|date=2014年1月22日 (水) 15:24 (UTC)}} {{神社 | 名称 = Hioki Shrine | 画像 = [[File:Hioki-jinja Tachibana ac (4).jpg|270px]]
Haiden | 所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Naka Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207921|qid=Q1207921|lt=Naka Ward|1=Naka-ku, Nagoya}}{{ill|lt=Tachibana (Nagoya)|Q29361704|qid=Q29361704|lt=Tachibana|1=Tachibana}} 1-3-21 | 位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|9|19|N|136|53|58|E|display=ti}} | 祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} |本殿 = {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} | 社格 = {{ill|lt=Junior First Rank Upper|Q11071121|qid=Q11071121|1=Junior First Rank}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|qid=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} | 別名 = Hioki Hachimangū
Hioki Tenjin
Hachimangū
Hachiman | 創建 = Unknown (possibly before the {{ill|lt=10th century|Q8052|qid=Q8052|1=10th century}}) | 例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|qid=Q2919|1=October 15}} | 地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Hioki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{Location map|Nagoya|label=Hioki Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Hioki Shrine''' (ひおきじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Tachibana 1-chōme, {{ill|lt=Naka Ward (Nagoya)|Q1207921|qid=Q1207921|lt=Naka Ward|1=Naka-ku, Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Nagoya|Q11751|qid=Q11751|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|qid=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}. It is also called "Hioki Hachimangū." It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} identified with "Hioki Shrine" of Aichi District, Owari Province, in the {{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|qid=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}. === Enshrined deities === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|qid=Q11059539|lt=Ame-no-Futodama-no-Mikoto|1=Futodama}} (あめのふとだまのみこと) * Left co-enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Homuda-wake-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (ほむだわけのみこと) * Right co-enshrined deity: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Amaterasu Kōtaijin|1=UNKNOWN}} (あまてらすすめおほかみ) === History === Foundation is unknown. Until the early modern period it was called '''Hachimangū''' and was the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|qid=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of Hioki village in Aichi District. In the mid-Edo period, {{ill|lt=Amano Nobukage|Q6535030|qid=Q6535030|1=Amano Sadakage}} identified this shrine with the "Hioki Shrine" of the Engishiki Jinmyōchō because it was located in Hioki village, and this became accepted theory. There are no differing theories identifying this shrine as Hioki Shrine; even late-Edo scholar {{ill|lt=Tsuda Masao|Q22123474|qid=Q22123474|1=Tsuda Masanari}} regarded it as Hioki Shrine. A wooden tablet has been excavated from {{ill|lt=Heijō-kyō|Q862509|qid=Q862509|1=Heijō-kyō}} bearing the text "Hioki-ri, Kusabe-gō, Aichi District, Owari Province," suggesting this area was called "Hioki-ri" in the {{ill|lt=Nara period|Q189178|qid=Q189178|1=Nara period}}. The name "Hioki" has many unclear points, but one theory derives it from the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hioki-be|1=UNKNOWN}} who managed calendars. Around the shrine, several ancient sites are confirmed, including the {{ill|lt=Ōsu Kofun group|Q133820892|qid=Q133820892|1=Ōsu Kofun Group}} and {{ill|lt=Hatago-chō site (Nagoya)|Q30926880|qid=Q30926880|lt=Hatagochō archaeological site|1=Hatagochō archaeological site}}. For divine rank, the Atsuta manuscript of the {{ill|lt=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō|Q11041830|qid=Q11041830|1=Owari Kokunai Jinmyōchō}} lists "Junior First Rank Upper, Hioki Tenjin," while the Kōnomiya manuscript has "Junior Third Rank, Hioki Tenjin." In the medieval period, it is said Hachiman Ōkami was invited from Otokoyama in {{ill|lt=Yamashiro Province|Q749276|qid=Q749276|1=Yamashiro Province}} and jointly enshrined (possibly also connected to {{ill|lt=Wakamiya Hachimangū-sha|Q11616253|qid=Q11616253|1=Wakamiya Hachiman-gū Shrine (Kyoto)}} at Kyoto Higashiyama Gojō). In the fifth month of {{ill|lt=Eiroku|Q1143341|qid=Q1143341|1=Eiroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1560|Q6613|qid=Q6613|1=1560}}), when {{ill|lt=Oda Nobunaga|Q171411|qid=Q171411|1=Oda Nobunaga}} departed for the {{ill|lt=Battle of Okehazama|Q349422|qid=Q349422|1=Battle of Okehazama}}, he left Kiyosu Castle at dawn, came to this shrine, performed "Atsumori" and prayed for victory, then moved to {{ill|lt=Atsuta Jingū|Q482065|qid=Q482065|1=Atsuta Shrine}} after his forces assembled. After his victory, he planted one thousand pine trees in the sacred precinct as thanksgiving; since then it was called "Senbonmatsu Hioki Hachimangū." Until before WWII, the shrine possessed munafuda with dates such as {{ill|lt=Bunroku|Q1373039|qid=Q1373039|1=Bunroku}} 1 ({{ill|lt=1592|Q6769|qid=Q6769|1=1592}}) and {{ill|lt=Keichō|Q1328185|qid=Q1328185|1=Keichō}} 13 ({{ill|lt=1608|Q6758|qid=Q6758|1=1608}}), indicating multiple rebuilding phases from Sengoku through Edo. The early-Edo ''{{ill|lt=Kanbun Muramura Oboegaki|Q137766629|qid=Q137766629|1=Kanbunmuramuraoboegaki}}'' records "Hioki village... Hachiman ... Atsuta negi Oritaifu ... exempt from before," showing it was under Atsuta negi Ōhara Oritaifu. The nearby Shichimenzan Myōzen-ji appears to have been built by carving out shrine land; records remain of annual land-tax payments as ground rent to Ōhara Oritaifu. At the {{ill|lt=Meiji Restoration|Q8707|qid=Q8707|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the shrine name was changed to "Hioki Shrine." In {{ill|lt=1871|Q7746|qid=Q7746|1=1871}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|qid=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 4), it was promoted to gōsha. During {{ill|lt=shinbutsu bunri|Q1970535|qid=Q1970535|1=Shinbutsu bunri}}, shrine families resigned with source materials, resulting in dispersal of records. In {{ill|lt=1896|Q7846|qid=Q7846|1=1896}} (Meiji 29), the last of the thousand pines reportedly died. On Dec 26, {{ill|lt=1907|Q2048|qid=Q2048|1=1907}} (Meiji 40), it was designated a shinsen-heihaku-ryō kyōshin shrine. In Oct {{ill|lt=1909|Q2057|qid=Q2057|1=1909}} (Meiji 42), neighboring village Shinmei-sha was merged and co-enshrined. In Mar {{ill|lt=1945|Q5240|qid=Q5240|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20), it was damaged in the {{ill|lt=Nagoya Air Raid|Q2813858|qid=Q2813858|1=Bombing of Nagoya}}. In {{ill|lt=1958|Q5253|qid=Q5253|1=1958}} (Shōwa 33), restoration and rebuilding of shrine buildings was completed. === Precincts === * Shrine buildings * Shrine office * {{ill|lt=Chikaraishi|Q1660393|qid=Q1660393|1=Chikaraishi}} * Torii === Subsidiary shrines === * Inari-sha * Daikoku-Ebisu-sha * Tachibana-sha * Tsushima-sha / Atsuta-sha / Akiba-sha combined hall * Kagutsuchi-sha / Susanoo-sha combined hall * Kagutsuchi-sha * Ryūjin-sha Hioki-jinja Tachibana ac (2).jpg|Precincts Hioki-jinja Tachibana ac (9).jpg|Chikaraishi Hioki-jinja Tachibana ac (5).jpg|Oni-gawara roof tile === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=January 1|Q2150|qid=Q2150|1=January 1}}: Saitan-sai * {{ill|lt=January 10|Q2243|qid=Q2243|1=January 10}}: Daikoku/Ebisu festival (Hatsu-ebisu) * {{ill|lt=February 11|Q2335|qid=Q2335|1=February 11}}: {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Kigen Festival|1=UNKNOWN}} * {{ill|lt=February 17|Q2341|qid=Q2341|1=February 17}}: Kinen-sai * Old lunar 2nd month first horse day: Inari Hatsuuma festival * {{ill|lt=June 30|Q2657|qid=Q2657|1=June 30}}: {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|qid=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} * {{ill|lt=July 15|Q2686|qid=Q2686|1=July 15}}: Kinkō rite / Chinowa rite * {{ill|lt=July 26|Q2723|qid=Q2723|1=July 26}}: Ryūjin festival * {{ill|lt=October 15|Q2919|qid=Q2919|1=October 15}}: Annual festival * {{ill|lt=November 23|Q3021|qid=Q3021|1=November 23}}: {{ill|lt=Niiname-sai|Q11501518|qid=Q11501518|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} * {{ill|lt=December 31|Q2912|qid=Q2912|1=December 31}}: {{ill|lt=Ōharae|Q11261910|qid=Q11261910|1=Ōharae-shiki}} * Every month on the 1st and 15th: {{ill|lt=Tsukinami-sai|Q11516161|qid=Q11516161|1=Tsukinami-no-Matsuri}} === Footnotes === {{Reflist}} ===
